title: श्री सूक्तम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूक्तम् ॥

ॐ हिरण्यवर्णां हरिणीं सुवर्णरजतस्रजाम्।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह॥ १॥

तां म आवह जातवेदो लक्ष्मीमनपगामिनीम्।
यस्यां हिरण्यं विन्देयं गामश्वं पुरुषानहम्॥ २॥

अश्वपूर्वां रथमध्यां हस्तिनादप्रबोधिनीम्।
श्रियं देवीमुपह्वये श्रीर्मा देवी जुषताम्॥ ३॥

कां सोस्मितां हिरण्यप्राकारामार्द्रां ज्वलन्तीं तृप्तां तर्पयन्तीम्।
पद्मे स्थितां पद्मवर्णां तामिहोपह्वये श्रियम्॥ ४॥

चन्द्रां प्रभासां यशसा ज्वलन्तीं श्रियं लोके देवजुष्टामुदाराम्।
तां पद्मिनीमीं शरणमहं प्रपद्येऽलक्ष्मीर्मे नश्यतां त्वां वृणे॥ ५॥

आदित्यवर्णे तपसोऽधिजातो वनस्पतिस्तव वृक्षोऽथ बिल्वः।
तस्य फलानि तपसानुदन्तु मायान्तरायाश्च बाह्या अलक्ष्मीः॥ ६॥

उपैतु मां देवसखः कीर्तिश्च मणिना सह।
प्रादुर्भूतोऽस्मि राष्ट्रेऽस्मिन् कीर्तिमृद्धिं ददातु मे॥ ७॥

क्षुत्पिपासामलां ज्येष्ठामलक्ष्मीं नाशयाम्यहम्।
अभूतिमसमृद्धिं च सर्वां निर्णुद मे गृहात्॥ ८॥

गन्धद्वारां दुराधर्षां नित्यपुष्टां करीषिणीम्।
ईश्वरीं सर्वभूतानां तामिहोपह्वये श्रियम्॥ ९॥

मनसः काममाकूतिं वाचः सत्यमशीमहि।
पशूनां रूपमन्नस्य मयि श्रीः श्रयतां यशः॥ १०॥

कर्दमेन प्रजाभूता मयि सम्भव कर्दम।
श्रियं वासय मे कुले मातरं पद्ममालिनीम्॥ ११॥

आपः सृजन्तु स्निग्धानि चिक्लीत वस मे गृहे।
नि च देवीं मातरं श्रियं वासय मे कुले॥ १२॥

आर्द्रां पुष्करिणीं पुष्टिं पिङ्गलां पद्ममालिनीम्।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह॥ १३॥

आर्द्रां यः करिणीं यष्टिं सुवर्णां हेममालिनीम्।
सूर्यां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह॥ १४॥

तां म आवह जातवेदो लक्ष्मीमनपगामिनीम्।
यस्यां हिरण्यं प्रभूतं गावो दास्योऽश्वान् विन्देयं पुरुषानहम्॥ १५॥

यः शुचिः प्रयतो भूत्वा जुहुयादाज्यमन्वहम्।
श्रियः पञ्चदशर्चं च श्रीकामः सततं जपेत्॥

पद्मानने पद्मविपद्मपत्रे पद्मदलायताक्षि।
विश्वप्रिये विष्णुमनोऽनुकूले त्वत्पादपद्मं मयि सन्निधत्स्व॥

॥ ॐ महालक्ष्म्यै च विद्महे विष्णुपत्न्यै च धीमहि।
तन्नो लक्ष्मीः प्रचोदयात् ॥

ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Suktam ||

Om Hiranya-varnam harinim suvarna-rajata-srajam |
Chandram hiranmayim Lakshmim Jatavedo ma avaha || 1 ||

Tam ma avaha Jatavedo Lakshmi-anapagaminim |
Yasyam hiranyam vindeyam gam-ashvam purusham-aham || 2 ||

Ashva-purvam ratha-madhyam hasti-nada-prabodhinim |
Shriyam Devim-upahvaye Shrir-ma Devi jushatam || 3 ||

Kam sosmitam hiranya-prakaram-ardram jvalantim triptam tarpayantim |
Padme sthitam padma-varnam tam-ihopahvaye Shriyam || 4 ||

Chandram prabhasam yashasa jvalantim shriyam loke deva-jushtam-udaram |
Tam padminim-im sharanam-aham prapadye-Alakshmirme nashyatam tvam vrine || 5 ||

Aditya-varne tapaso-adhijato vanaspatistava vriksho-atha bilvah |
Tasya phalani tapasa-nudantu maya-antarayashcha bahya Alakshmih || 6 ||

Upaitu mam deva-sakhah kirtishcha manina saha |
Pradurbhuto-asmi rashtre-asmin kirtim-riddhim dadatu me || 7 ||

Kshut-pipasa-amalam jyeshtham-Alakshmim nashayamy-aham |
Abhutim-asamriddhim cha sarvam nirnuda me grihat || 8 ||

Gandha-dvaram duradharshaam nitya-pushtam karishinim |
Ishvarim sarva-bhutanam tam-ihopahvaye Shriyam || 9 ||

Manasah kamam-akutim vachah satyam-ashimahi |
Pashunam rupam-annasya mayi Shrih shrayatam yashah || 10 ||

Kardamena praja-bhuta mayi sambhava Kardama |
Shriyam vasaya me kule mataram padma-malinim || 11 ||

Apah srijantu snigdhani chiklita vasa me grihe |
Ni cha devim mataram Shriyam vasaya me kule || 12 ||

Ardram pushkarinim pushtim pingalam padma-malinim |
Chandram hiranmayim Lakshmim Jatavedo ma avaha || 13 ||

Ardram yah karinim yashtim suvarnam hema-malinim |
Suryam hiranmayim Lakshmim Jatavedo ma avaha || 14 ||

Tam ma avaha Jatavedo Lakshmi-anapagaminim |
Yasyam hiranyam prabhutam gavo dasyo-ashvan vindeyam purusham-aham || 15 ||

Yah shuchih prayato bhutva juhuyad-ajyam-anvaham |
Shriyah panchadashar-cham cha Shrikamah satatam japet ||

Padmanane padma-vipadma-patre padma-dalayatakshi |
Vishvapriye Vishnu-mano-anukule tvat-pada-padmam mayi sannidhatsva ||

|| Om Mahalakshmyai cha vidmahe Vishnu-patnyai cha dhimahi |
Tanno Lakshmih prachodayat ||

Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||
hindiMeaning:
हे जातवेद (अग्नि)! स्वर्ण वर्ण वाली, हरित शोभा वाली, सुवर्ण और रजत की मालाओं वाली, चन्द्रमा के समान कान्तिमयी, स्वर्णमयी लक्ष्मी को मेरे पास लाइए॥ १॥

हे जातवेद! उन लक्ष्मी को मेरे पास लाइए जो कभी न जाने वाली हैं, जिनमें मुझे सुवर्ण, गौ, अश्व और पुरुष (सेवक) प्राप्त हों॥ २॥

अश्वों से आगे, रथ के मध्य में, हाथियों की ध्वनि से जागृत करने वाली श्री देवी को मैं बुलाता हूँ। देवी श्री मुझे स्वीकार करें॥ ३॥

मुस्कुराती हुई, स्वर्ण प्राकार (किले) वाली, स्निग्ध, ज्वलन्ती, तृप्त और तृप्ति देने वाली, कमल पर स्थित, कमल वर्ण वाली श्री को मैं यहाँ बुलाता हूँ॥ ४॥

चन्द्रमा के समान, प्रभावती, यश से दीप्तिमान, लोक में देवताओं द्वारा पूजित, उदार श्री-पद्मिनी की मैं शरण लेता हूँ। मेरी अलक्ष्मी (दरिद्रता) नष्ट हो, मैं आपको वरण करता हूँ॥ ५॥

हे सूर्य वर्ण वाली! आपके तप से उत्पन्न वनस्पति, वृक्ष और बिल्व हैं। उसके फल तप से (अलक्ष्मी को) दूर भगाएँ, भीतरी और बाहरी अलक्ष्मी नष्ट हो॥ ६॥

सुगन्ध के द्वार वाली, दुर्जय, नित्य पुष्ट, उपजाऊ, सर्व प्राणियों की ईश्वरी श्री को मैं यहाँ बुलाता हूँ॥ ९॥

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीसूक्तमहामन्त्रस्य आनन्दकर्दमचिक्लीतश्रीपुत्र ऋषयः। अनुष्टुप् त्रिष्टुप् छन्दांसि। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता। ॐ श्रीं बीजम्। श्रीशक्तिः शक्तिः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रीत्यर्थे श्रीसूक्तजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of poverty (alakshmi) from home and life
Traditionally associated with gold, cattle, and material comforts
Invokes the blessing of fame, reputation, and social status
Invokes the permanent grace of goddess lakshmi
Traditionally recited for removal of financial obstacles and debts
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness and good fortune to the family
Highly effective when chanted during Diwali and Fridays
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Shri Suktam (श्री सूक्तम्) is a Vedic hymn revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Rigveda - Khilani (Appendix to Rigveda Samhita) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to bring gold, cattle, and material comforts and remove financial obstacles and debts. It is also said to bestow fame, reputation, and social status. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar), Friday (Shukravar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi and Diwali. The text contains 15 verses and takes around 10 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shri Suktam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shri Suktam from Rigveda in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Vedic hymn to Goddess Lakshmi for wealth and prosperity.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shri Suktam and what is its source in the Vedas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shri Suktam (श्री सूक्तम्) is the foremost Vedic hymn to Goddess Lakshmi, preserved in the Khilani (Khila — appendix) of the Rigveda Samhita, traditionally numbered as the 2.6th Khila Sukta of the Rigveda. The hymn comprises 15 verses (with two preliminary and concluding verses making 17 in many liturgical recitations), each invoking Devi Shri (Lakshmi) as the goddess of wealth, gold, cattle, food, prosperity, beauty and well-being, and praying for her presence (avahana) and her permanent residence (sthapana) in the home of the worshipper. It is the most-recited Vedic hymn in Lakshmi worship across Hindu traditions and is the third Sukta in the Pancha Suktam parayanam. (Sources: Rigveda Khilani 2.6; classical Shri Sukta tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Hiranya-varnam Hari-nim Suvarna-rajata-srajam?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated opening verse of the Shri Suktam invokes Devi Lakshmi: Hiranya-varnam — of golden complexion (signifying solar radiance, purity and the precious nature of consciousness); Hari-nim — green-yellow like the doe (representing fresh vegetation, fertility and abundance); Suvarna-rajata-srajam — adorned with garlands of gold and silver (signifying that all wealth is her ornament); Chandram — moon-like (cooling, peaceful, gracious); Hiranmayim — golden-bodied; Lakshmim jatavedo ma avaha — O Jatavedas (Agni, the messenger of the gods), bring Lakshmi to me. The verse establishes Lakshmi's iconographic descriptors as cosmic principles — gold is not merely metal but consciousness, vegetation is not merely plant but the living force, and her presence is invoked through Agni the cosmic messenger. (Sources: Rigveda Khilani 2.6.1; classical Shri Sukta exegesis.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Shri Suktam most powerfully recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shri Suktam is most powerfully recited on Diwali (Kartik Amavasya — the great Lakshmi Puja night), Sharad Purnima (Kojagari Lakshmi Puja in Ashwin), Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya — the never-decreasing day for gold purchase and Lakshmi worship), Varalakshmi Vrata (Shravana Shukla second Friday — the great south Indian Lakshmi vrata), every Friday (Shukravar — the day of Shukra and Lakshmi), every Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu-Lakshmi day), every Purnima (full moon, sacred to Lakshmi), Dhanteras (the day before Diwali), all Vishnu festivals, and during Pancha Suktam parayanam (where Shri Sukta is the third Sukta). The most classical practice is daily Shri Sukta parayanam at sunset facing east with a lit ghee deepam. (Sources: Drikpanchang Lakshmi festival calendar; classical Shri Sukta tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How should the Shri Suktam be recited for wealth and prosperity?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Shri Sukta upasana paddhati for prosperity prescribes the following vidhi: (1) Bathe in the early morning, wear clean red, pink, yellow or gold-coloured clothes (Lakshmi's colours); (2) Sit on a clean asana of red or yellow cloth facing east or northeast; (3) Place a picture, murti or yantra of Devi Lakshmi (especially Sri Yantra or Lakshmi-Narayana together) on a chowki with a red cloth; (4) Light a pure ghee lamp (preferably with a single wick of cotton); (5) Offer red flowers (especially lotus and red rose), kumkum, akshat (rice grains), sandalwood paste, betel leaves with betel nut, fruits (especially banana), and a naivedya of kheer or milk-sweets; (6) Recite the Shri Suktam 1, 3, 5 or 11 times with shraddha; (7) Conclude with Lakshmi Beej Mantra Om Shrim Mahalakshmyai Namaha 108 times; (8) Distribute prasada and offer dana to Brahmins or the needy. (Sources: classical Shri Sukta upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Shri Suktam and other Lakshmi stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: Shri Suktam is uniquely Vedic (from the Rigveda Khila itself) and therefore carries the highest scriptural authority of all Lakshmi prayers. Other major Lakshmi stotras have different sources and roles: Lakshmi Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names from the Padma Purana — devotional litany), Lakshmi Sahasranama (1000 names — comprehensive worship), Mahalakshmi Ashtakam (Indra's hymn from Padma Purana — eight-verse Mahalakshmi praise), Kanakadhara Stotram (Adi Shankara's hymn for instant gold-rain, 18-22 verses), Lakshmi Stuti by Indra (in various Puranas), and the Sri Suktam and Bhu Suktam pair (the Vedic Pancha Suktam component). Shri Suktam is the foundational Vedic Lakshmi mantra; the others are devotional, Puranic and tantric elaborations. The Shri Sukta is recited first in any Lakshmi puja sequence. (Sources: Rigveda Khila; Padma Purana; classical Lakshmi stotra anthology.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to Shri Suktam parayanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to regular Shri Suktam parayanam: bestowal of all eight forms of Lakshmi (Adi Lakshmi, Dhana Lakshmi, Dhanya Lakshmi, Gaja Lakshmi, Santana Lakshmi, Veera Lakshmi, Vijaya Lakshmi, Vidya Lakshmi — Ashta-Lakshmi), abundance of gold and material wealth, abundance of food grain (annam) and prosperity in agriculture, increase of cattle, vehicles and modern conveyances, blessings of fertility and progeny (Santana Lakshmi), success in business, trade and entrepreneurship, removal of poverty (daridra-nashana), permanent residence of Lakshmi in the home (sthira-Lakshmi), removal of debts and litigation losses, beauty and grace (saundarya), success in marriage and family harmony (Lakshmi being inseparable from Narayana — household harmony), and the supreme phala — moksha at the end of life through Vishnu-Lakshmi grace. (Sources: Shri Sukta phalashruti; Vaishnava paddhati; Lakshmi tantra.)
